import io
import string
import list

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
end

fn flush(_ acc: List<Int>, _ cur: String) -> List<Int>
  if string.is_empty(cur)
    acc
  else
    match string.parse_int(cur)
    when Some(v)
      list.push(acc, v)
    when None
      acc
    end
  end
end

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut cur = ""
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if is_ws(b)
        result = flush(result, cur)
        cur = ""
      else
        cur = "#{cur}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result = flush(result, cur)
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ arr: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = arr.len() - 1
  mut found: Option<Int> = None
  mut done = false
  while not done and lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match arr.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        found = Some(mid)
        done = true
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      done = true
    end
  end
  found
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
  match bsearch(nums, target)
  when Some(i)
    println("index=#{i}")
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
end
